sentence_reviews.text: Expert and production-rule systems apply explicit if–then rules over symbolic facts, separating a curated knowledge base from an inference engine.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA knowledge-based agents, inference, and expert-systems material describes knowledge bases, inference engines, and rule-based reasoning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The standard expert-system architecture is supported; the exact combined wording is synthesized from AIMA topics.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is to connect Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution to downstream capabilities including Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning, Verified and assurance-gated agents.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports knowledge representation, rules, logic, and inference; it does not directly cover semantic search or assurance-gated agents in this packet.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core symbolic/logic dependencies are supported, but the downstream capability list is broad graph synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA knowledge representation and reasoning material supports symbolic relational reasoning, not semantic search specifically.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relational reasoning is supported; semantic search and the exact substrate relationship need narrower evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Verified and assurance-gated agents
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA covers knowledge-based agents and logical inference but not verified or assurance-gated agent systems as stated.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited evidence is insufficient for the verification/assurance-gated downstream claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports rule/knowledge/inference dependencies; the packet does not cite evidence for the generic limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are plausible; limitation wording is generic and not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines consumes, emits, or organizes information using Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA knowledge representation and knowledge-base material supports symbolic schemas/facts for expert systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: Knowledge bases are directly relevant, but the modern knowledge-graph label is broader than the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA covers rule-based inference, logical reasoning, and constraint satisfaction topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Logic/inference support is clear; constraint-program execution is broader than expert systems alone.

sentence_reviews.text: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports propositional/Boolean logic for rule reasoning; differentiable logic gates are not directly part of classical expert systems in packet evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Boolean logic is relevant, but the dependency label includes differentiable gates, making the claim overbroad.

edge_reviews.notes: Knowledge-base/representation dependence is supported; strength core is defensible under the broad atlas label.
Rule inference and logic support the composes edge, though constraint-program execution is broader than the direct evidence.
Boolean/propositional logic is supported, but differentiable logic gates are not necessary for classical expert systems; common strength is plausible but relationship is overbroad.
